Description: Variables, functions, and files use clear, consistent naming (e.g., camelCase for C, snake_case for Python)
Functions, complex logic, and business rules have clear comments explaining purpose and logic

Checklist Item: No hardcoded values except for demo purposes
Description: Configuration values, connection strings, and constants are externalized
Functions/methods are single-purpose and can be reused across programs

Checklist Item: No dead code or unused variables
Description: All code paths are used and variables are referenced
Indentation, spacing, and style are consistent throughout
All SQL operations check sqlca.sqlcode and handle errors appropriately
All host variables are declared in EXEC SQL BEGIN/END DECLARE SECTION
Database connections are properly established and released
DECLARE, OPEN, FETCH, CLOSE cursor operations are properly paired
COMMIT/ROLLBACK used appropriately for data consistency
Host variable sizes match database column sizes to prevent truncation
EXEC SQL INCLUDE and other directives are properly used
All database operations wrapped in try/except blocks with specific error types
Connections and cursors are properly opened and closed in finally blocks
SQL queries use parameter binding to prevent SQL injection
Context managers (with statements) used where available
Standard library, third-party, and local imports properly grouped and ordered
Code uses Python 3 syntax and features, no Python 2 compatibility issues
Proper EXCEPTION blocks with WHEN clauses for different error types
COMMIT/ROLLBACK used appropriately in procedures and anonymous blocks
Input parameters are validated for null values and data types
Queries are optimized, no unnecessary operations in loops
Variables declared in appropriate scope, no global variable abuse
All SQL statements are syntactically correct and follow Oracle standards
Foreign key relationships and constraints are respected
SELECT statements use appropriate WHERE clauses and JOINs
Large data operations use appropriate batching or bulk methods
Row-level or table-level locking used as needed for concurrency
Queries leverage appropriate indexes for performance
Input parameters are properly sanitized and parameterized
Database passwords and sensitive data not hardcoded or logged
Database users have minimum required permissions
All user inputs are validated for type, length, and format
Error messages don't expose sensitive system information
Important operations are logged for security monitoring
All allocated resources are properly freed/released
For high-traffic applications, connection pooling is implemented
Long-running queries are optimized or run asynchronously
Code can handle increased load without significant performance degradation
Memory, CPU, and I/O usage is reasonable for the operations performed
New programs and features are documented in README.md
Complex algorithms and business logic are well-documented
Function/method signatures and parameters are documented
README includes execution examples for all programs
Common errors and troubleshooting steps are documented
All required software and libraries are documented
Individual functions/methods have corresponding tests
Database operations are tested with actual database connections
Programs handle various error conditions gracefully
Boundary conditions and unusual inputs are tested
Programs perform adequately under expected load
Programs work on different operating systems and environments
File I/O operations handle errors and edge cases properly
Files are accessed with correct read/write permissions
Text files use appropriate character encoding (UTF-8)
Programs can handle files of various sizes without memory issues
Concurrent file access is handled appropriately
All Pro*C programs compile without errors
All required libraries and tools are available
Automated build processes execute successfully
Steps to deploy and run programs are clear
Required environment variables and paths are documented
